Ad Text:
About the Company

This is a fast-growing Australian technology company operating at the cutting edge of applied AI. They work with large enterprises across a wide range of industries to solve complex, real-world operational problems, building AI-powered solutions from the ground up that are designed to create genuine, measurable business impact for the organisations they work with.

The culture here rewards curiosity, accountability, and bold thinking. Leadership is hands-on and accessible, and the team is made up of sharp, driven people who take their craft seriously. Growth is a priority, both for the business and for the people within it. If you want to do meaningful work in a space that is genuinely evolving at pace, this is an environment built for that.

** NB: Our client can only facilitate the onboarding for Australian Permanent Residents or Citizens / NZ Citizens. For this position you will need to be located in Sydney, Melbourne or Brisbane **

About the Role

As the business continues to scale its enterprise client base, they are looking to bring on an experienced Tech Lead to sit at the centre of their delivery capability. This is not an internal-facing engineering role. It is a client-facing leadership position where you will be the most senior technical voice on the engagements you lead.

You will take ownership of AI-powered solutions end to end, from early scoping conversations with enterprise clients through to production deployment. You will lead project squads that shift from engagement to engagement, working alongside developers, data scientists, and project managers to deliver AI systems that solve real business problems. The expectation is someone who is energised by that client-facing challenge, not just the engineering underneath it.

What You Will Be Doing

Lead the technical design and implementation of AI Co-Workers from concept through production deployment
Translate real business constraints into scalable AI and engineering solutions
Own system architecture across models, orchestration logic, APIs, data pipelines, and integrations
Lead technical decision-making for LLM usage, model selection, prompt design, evaluation, and monitoring
Guide AI Engineers through build, test, and release cycles
Partner closely with Engagement Leads to align technical execution with value delivery
Ensure engineering quality across performance, reliability, security, and maintainability
Identify technical risks early and drive pragmatic solutions in ambiguous environments
Contribute hands-on to critical parts of the build while enabling the team to move faster
Act as the primary technical point of contact for enterprise clients, managing stakeholder expectations and communicating complex technical concepts clearly to non-technical audiences

Skills / Requirements

Proven leadership of small to medium-sized teams delivering products in enterprise environments
Strong background in software engineering with applied AI or ML experience. You are a well-rounded engineer comfortable across multiple languages, not a Python-only specialist
Proficiency in Node.js and TypeScript, the core stack and a non-negotiable requirement
Hands-on experience with modern AI systems including LLMs, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), LangChain, LangGraph, and prompt engineering. Practical delivery experience, not just surface-level tool usage
Solid understanding of backend systems, APIs, data flows, and production architecture
Experience working within enterprise environments, comfortable navigating complex organisations, regulations, and existing legacy applications
Ability to reason about trade-offs across accuracy, latency, cost, scalability, and delivery speed
Experience guiding or mentoring engineers through complex builds, having led a team of 4 or more
Comfort explaining technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Bias toward ownership, learning, and shipping real systems
Curiosity about how AI systems behave in production, not just in theory

Job Title: AI Solution Engineer – Automation & Digital Value (Fixed Term)
Ad Text:
Build AI that matters. Ship it in weeks, not months.
Ryman Healthcare is one of Australasia's leading retirement living and aged care operators, with 49 villages across New Zealand and Australia. We're on a mission to deliver a world-class retirement experience for our residents and we're looking for an AI Solution Engineer to help us get there faster.

This is a hands-on role for someone who builds, not just advises. You'll identify high-impact AI and automation opportunities across the business, prototype solutions rapidly, and deploy them into production in focused sprints. If you're the kind of person who'd rather ship something real in two weeks than spend two months writing a strategy deck, keep reading. 

What you'll do
You'll work across the full problem-to-production lifecycle — discovering use cases with business teams, designing and building AI solutions within the Microsoft ecosystem, and deploying automation that delivers measurable value for our residents, teams, and operations. You'll also contribute to how we govern AI responsibly, ensuring we scale with confidence in a healthcare environment where getting it right matters.
Your work will span Copilot Studio, Azure AI, Power Platform, RAG architectures, LLM integration, and AI agents — but the tools are secondary to the outcomes. We care about what you deliver, not what you demo.

This is a full time, fixed term role through to the end of March 2027, with the potential to extend or become permanent. This role can be based in our Christchurch office in Russley or our Southbank, Melbourne office.

What we're looking for
Someone with 3–5 years of experience in AI solution engineering, applied AI, or intelligent automation who has actually shipped solutions into production, not just prototypes. You'll bring hands-on experience with the Microsoft AI ecosystem, a working knowledge of RAG patterns and prompt engineering, and the ability to build automation using Power Platform. Just as importantly, you'll know how to explain what you're building and why it matters to clinical, operational, and executive stakeholders.
A degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience. Microsoft certifications in Azure AI or Power Platform are a bonus.

What makes this role different
You won't be buried in a data science team running experiments. You'll be embedded in the Digital Solutions team, working directly with Business Analysts, Business Solution Analysts, and business stakeholders to find the problems worth solving and then solving them, fast. You'll operate with an equal mandate: deliver value fast and govern it well.

Why Ryman
We're pioneering in how we care for our residents, and we're bringing that same mindset to how we use technology. You'll be joining an organisation that's genuinely committed to AI — not as a buzzword, but as a practical tool to improve the lives of residents and the teams who support them. You'll also get competitive pay, additional wellness leave, flexible working, access to 60+ employee discounts, and the chance to do work that makes a difference.

If you're our kind of person, we'd love to hear from you.
At Ryman, we support an environment where everyone feels welcome to be themselves. We embrace diversity and celebrate individual uniqueness. Applications will be reviewed as they are received and interviews may take place prior to the close date.

SEL invents, designs, and builds digitais products and systems that protect power grids around the world. This technology prevents blackouts and enables customers to improve power system reliability and safety at a reduced cost. A 100 percent employee-owned company headquartered in Pullman, Washington, SEL has manufactured products in the United States since 1984 and now serves customers worldwide.

Our mission is simple: to make electric power safer, more reliable, and more economical

Provides solutions to a wide variety of increasingly difficult problems. Solutions are increasingly imaginative, thorough, and practical. Independently supports customers for at least two knowledge areas of their specialization field.

Contributes to product specifications, application and testing software, and literature.


Participates and presents at SEL marketing and sales activities, industry technical meetings, and conferences.

Teaches SEL University courses within core area.

Publishes multiple application guides, notes, template guides, software tools, and/or technical conference papers.

Provides assistance on products and applications to engineers, associates, and interns.

Regular contributions through customer feedback process, FAQ entries, IMI teams, and improving SELU training material. Identifies WCM initiatives and leads efforts to improve.

Follow and apply SEL Values, Principles of Operations, and World-Class Manufacturing principles.

Understand. Create. Simplify.

Other duties as assigned.

To be successful in this role you will have the following:

Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering (or equivalent engineering degree)

Typically 6+ years of experience in power systems, industry practices, and automation

Ability to write and speak English coherently

Excellent technical training, speaking, writing, and customer service skills

Strong communicator. Actively identifies and pursues ways to improve customer experience.

Experiments to find new solutions and encourages others to do the same.

Ability to gain the confidence and trust of others easily. Inspires, motivates, and encourages others.

Ability to successfully carry out tasks, planning, instruction, and leadership

Models high standards of honesty and integrity

Ability to learn new skills and assume new responsibilities

Capable of selecting, applying and teaching principles, theories, and concepts in specialization field.

Ready to make a difference?
Apply today and help us shape the future of engineering solutions across the APAC region!

To Apply for This Position:

Interviews will commence shortly, so if you have what it takes to be successful in this role, please apply now by providing your CV and a brief cover letter regarding your suitability for the role.

Job Title: Data Scientist - Production Gen AI - Sydney
Ad Text:
Data Scientist – Production ML & GenAI
 Sydney | Circa $120,000 + Super + Bonus | Hybrid (3 days office)

About the Role
We’re working with a large enterprise in Sydney looking for a hands-on Data Scientist with around 2–4 years of commercial experience who wants to move beyond notebooks and work directly on real production AI solutions.
This role sits within a delivery-focused environment where you’ll work closely with engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders to take machine learning and GenAI use cases (including GPT/LLM-based solutions) from prototype through to production.

Rather than sitting in a purely experimental function, you’ll be embedded in a cross-functional squad where the focus is on building, deploying, and supporting real-world data science solutions that are scalable, governed, and production-ready.
What You’ll Be Doing
Production & Engineering Collaboration
Work closely with engineers to help deploy and support ML & AI solutions in production
Assist in transitioning prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems
Support monitoring, debugging, and improving existing data pipelines and models
Gain exposure to real deployment workflows, CI/CD concepts, and release processes
Hands-on Data Science Work
Build and refine machine learning models and feature pipelines
Work with structured and unstructured data across multiple use cases
Support model evaluation, testing, and performance tracking
Contribute to experimentation, statistical analysis, and model explainability
Rapid Prototyping & Experimentation
Develop quick prototypes to test ideas, feasibility, and data availability
Translate business problems into testable hypotheses and simple models
Run experiments, compare approaches, and iterate based on results
Clearly document findings and recommendations for productionisation
Governance & Responsible AI
Work within established model governance and risk frameworks
Support documentation of model assumptions, limitations, and validation results
Ensure solutions meet data privacy, compliance, and enterprise standards
Collaboration & Growth
Act as a bridge between data science, engineering, and business teams
Share insights and contribute to improving team practices and standards
Support and learn from senior team members in a collaborative environment
About You
We’re looking for someone early in their Data Science career (2-4 years) who is ready to step into production-focused work.

You’ll likely have:

2–4 years’ experience as a Data Scientist or similar role
Strong Python and SQL skills
GPT Model experience (or similar) 
Experience working with machine learning models in a commercial environment
Exposure to model deployment or production systems (even if limited)
Microsoft Azure experience 
Nice to have:

Exposure to GenAI / LLMs (e.g. GPT, Azure OpenAI, NLP projects)
Understanding of model monitoring, validation, or drift detection
Experience working in regulated or enterprise environments
Why This Role?
Real opportunity to work on production ML and GenAI systems, not just experimentation
Exposure to modern Azure-based AI tooling and GPT/LLM use cases
Embedded in a delivery squad working end-to-end on real business problems
Strong mentorship and learning from senior engineers and data scientists
Clear career growth pathway.
Location & Package
Sydney based
Hybrid working (3 days in office per week)
$120,000 + Superannuation
Full-time permanent role

blueAPACHE is an Australian-owned Managed Service Provider, recognised as Mid-Market Partner of the Year at the ARN Innovation Awards for six consecutive years. We support some of Australia’s most complex mid-market businesses — including top-tier law firms, major retailers, mining companies, and pharma organisations — with infrastructure and cloud services that run at enterprise depth.

Our Portfolio Engineers are the most senior technical people our customers interact with. They are trusted advisors, escalation owners, and technical leaders — not ticket handlers. We hire for depth, autonomy, and judgment.

The Role

This is a senior, autonomous, client-facing engineering role. You will own a dedicated portfolio of mid-market customers — acting as their primary technical advisor, highest escalation point, and strategic infrastructure lead. You will be expected to operate independently across complex, multi-tenant environments, lead pre-sales conversations, deliver end-to-end projects, and drive proactive outcomes without needing a team around you to reach a conclusion.

Key Responsibilities

Client Ownership & Advisory

Own the technical relationship for your assigned customer portfolio — including regular strategic meetings, QBRs, and roadmap discussions with senior stakeholders.

Act as the primary technical contact for customers, Service Delivery Managers, Pre-Sales, and Projects teams — independently and with authority.

Create and present multi-year technology roadmaps aligned to each customer’s business objectives.

Drive solution onboarding and new service adoption into Managed Services for your customer group.

Participate in pre-sales activities including scoping, solution design, effort estimation, and client-facing presentations.

L3 Escalation & Technical Leadership

Serve as the highest technical escalation point for complex infrastructure incidents, problems, and changes across your portfolio.

Own P1/critical incident response end-to-end: triage, resolution, customer communication, and post-incident review.

Provide clear technical direction to BAU and L2/L3 engineers — guiding them to resolution, not just observing.

Perform root cause and trend analysis to proactively reduce incident noise before it impacts customers.

Adhere to Incident, Problem, and Change Management processes in accordance with ITIL standards.

Proactive Infrastructure & Project Delivery

Audit and uplift proactive infrastructure activity across your portfolio, categorising work into BAU, billable, and value-add initiatives (health checks, optimisation, security hardening, capacity management).

Deliver small-to-medium scale technical projects end-to-end — from audit and design through to implementation, documentation, and service desk handover.

Identify and act on opportunities for infrastructure uplift, security improvement, and modernisation within your customer environments.

Produce accurate AsBuilt documentation, proposals, and implementation plans to a professional standard.

Team & Knowledge Contribution

Mentor and upskill L2/L3 engineers, sharing technical knowledge and guiding them through complex client and infrastructure scenarios.

Contribute to internal tooling, templates, standards, and process improvement initiatives.

Act as a company-wide SME for technologies within your area of depth.

What We’re Looking For

Experience

10+ years of hands-on infrastructure experience, with the majority in an MSP or Systems Integrator environment.

Proven track record of independently owning client relationships — including strategic meetings, roadmaps, and escalation ownership.

Demonstrated experience delivering complex, end-to-end projects: audit, scoping, design, implementation, documentation, and handover.

Active involvement in pre-sales: solution design, effort estimation, and client-facing technical presentations.

Mentorship and technical leadership of junior engineering staff.

Confident communicator at all levels — from service desk engineers to C-suite stakeholders and boards.

Strong documentation discipline — proposals, AsBuilts, runbooks, and roadmaps produced to a professional standard.

Technical Depth

Microsoft Azure (IaaS, PaaS, Security) — including advanced identity, hybrid connectivity, and governance.

Identity platforms: Entra ID, Active Directory, ADFS, Azure AD B2B/B2C; and optionally Okta or CyberArk.

M365 Modern Workplace: Exchange Online, Intune, Autopilot, AVD/W365, Defender suite, DLP, Conditional Access.

On-premises infrastructure: Windows Server, Hyper-V, VMware vSphere/ESXi, SAN/NAS storage, failover clustering.

Networking: firewalls (Palo Alto, Fortinet, SonicWall, Cisco), switching, routing, VLANs, VPN, QoS, load balancing.

Scripting and automation: PowerShell, Bash; infrastructure automation mindset strongly preferred.

Backup and DR platforms: Veeam, Azure Site Recovery, Commvault or equivalent.

Security frameworks and uplift: Essential 8, ISO 27001, Microsoft Defender stack, CVE remediation.

Certifications

Current AZ-104 (Azure Administrator Associate) or AZ-305

Vendor certifications (e.g. PCNSE, NSE, VMware VCP, MCSE, CompTIA Security+).

ITIL Foundation.

Benefits

blueAPACHE continues to grow alongside some of Australia’s leading midmarket businesses, our valued clients. We continue to stay abreast of current technologies to maintain our competitive advantage, offering the opportunity to continually expand your technical expertise and provide an uncompressing offering to our clients. We recognise our people are our most significant differentiator.

We offer options for you to interview virtually.

blueAPACHE oﬀers an environment to make a real diﬀerence, develop careers within a supportive and rewarding environment.

Regular social events and an awesome culture – work with a team of experts in their field

Health Insurance discount with Medibank

Discounts on 1000’s of products

Employee Assistance Program from The Mind Room

Ongoing training and development including paid certifications.

Quality hardware and laptop provided.

Employee referral programs - once you are in and you are loving it - you can refer a friend and get paid for it!

*** Please note, all offers of employment at blueAPACHE are subject to National Police Checking Service.

We're seeking a Research & Market Intelligence Analyst to join our Milwaukee‑based team and support data‑driven business decisions across the organization. In this role, you'll apply a strong understanding of forecasting techniques, financial reporting, commercial performance metrics, and economic indicators to analyse market dynamics and inform strategic planning.

You'll synthesize insights from internal performance data, government statistics and regulatory datasets, census information, and industry research, translating complex analyses into clear narratives and recommendations for executive and senior stakeholder audiences. Your work will play a key role in informing market planning, investment decisions, and long‑term growth strategy.

What your day-today look like:

Build, maintain, and refine forecasting models to support business planning by validating assumptions, reconciling data sources, and clearly documenting methodologies.
Conduct market sizing analyses (TAM, SAM, SOM) to quantify market opportunities to support strategic and commercial decisions by integrating internal performance data with external market, economic, and regulatory datasets.
Source, interpret, and analyse government and regulatory data across Australia and New Zealand, including datasets from ABS, Stats NZ, and ASIC, to support forecasting, market sizing, and strategic analysis.

Develop, maintain, and integrate analytical databases into Qlik Sense dashboards using SQL for data extraction and transformation and Python for data preparation, ensuring accurate data models, reliable refresh processes, and reporting.
Identify opportunities to use AI tools to automate or streamline repetitive analysis, data preparation and reporting tasks, improving speed, consistency and insight turnaround.

What you will bring to the role:

Working knowledge of Python/ SQL and hands on experience designing, developing, and maintaining analytical databases and dashboards to support reporting and forecasting.
Solid understanding of forecasting techniques and experience interpreting financial reports, commercial metrics, and economic indicators.
Interest in and experience with applying AI tools to research, analytics or insight generation, with an ability to critically assess and validate AI generated outputs.
A highly organized self-starter with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Ability to think laterally to help identify alternate options and develop innovative solutions.

If you fit the above attributes and are excited at the prospect of joining a large, successful, and dynamic organisation, then please click APPLY NOW!

Job Title: Lead Software Engineer - AI Platform Services
Ad Text:
Company Description

About SEEK

Job Description

About the role

You will lead the engineering function within the All the Ads team, part of SEEK's Pricing and Performance group. You will design and deliver AI/ML systems, data assets, and pipelines that help SEEK better understand hirers and job ads at scale. You will collaborate with data scientists and AI product managers across three problem domains, managing a team of engineers while driving technical excellence and rapid delivery. You will own system architecture, mature ML-Ops practices, and ensure smooth integration with partner teams across the business. Success means building high-performing cross-functional teams, meeting delivery roadmaps, and advancing AI capabilities that directly impact millions of users.

Lead cross-functional team to apply latest AI/ML practices

Manage, coach, and grow engineers through line management
Collaborate with data science, product, and business stakeholders on key decisions
Oversee integration of backend services with partner teams
Qualifications

10+ years backend software engineering with strong technical skills
Proficient in Python and/or Java (required)

Proven delivery and people management experience

Commercian Implementation & Service Engineer - Australia Full-Time | Field-Based | Nationwide & International Travel

About Dexory

Dexory is redefining how the logistics industry operates. Our autonomous robots and real-time data platform give warehouse operators unprecedented visibility into their operations; tracking, measuring and locating goods at speed and scale, while building a live digital twin of every facility we work in.

Backed by some of Europe's leading VCs and working with major players in global logistics, we're in a period of rapid growth. We're now expanding our presence in Australia, and we're looking for someone exceptional to lead this charge on the ground.

The Role

This is a dual-function position designed for someone who thrives in the field. You'll begin as our primary Deployment Engineer in Australia — owning end-to-end installation and commissioning of Dexory's robotic systems at client warehouses — then evolve into an ongoing Service Engineer role, maintaining and repairing the fleet you've helped build out.

⚠️ Travel requirement: This role is approximately 100% field-based across Australia, with occasional international travel. A valid Australian driver's licence and unrestricted passport are essential.

Because of the travel side of the role, we are not able to consider those on work visas currently.

What You'll Do

Deployment

Be the primary customer contact for installation and commissioning of Dexory's robotic platforms at warehouse sites across Australia

Plan and coordinate site visits, incorporating mechanical, electrical, software, and network components

Execute on-site system setup, calibration, configuration, and testing in line with project specifications

Develop and maintain clear installation, operational, and troubleshooting documentation

Work closely with commercial and engineering teams to ensure seamless handover from sale to live deployment

Service & Maintenance

Perform on-site servicing, fault-finding, and repairs for Dexory robots across your client base

Develop and implement service plans aligned with engineering recommendations

Maintain product traceability records and ensure compliance with quality assurance processes

Respond to field issues proactively to minimise client downtime

Feed real-world learnings back to engineering and product teams

What We're Looking For

Essential:

Hands-on background with autonomous hardware, robotics, computer vision, or LiDAR-based systems - this is highly valued

5+ years of electromechanical assembly, servicing and repair from a precision industry (aerospace, automotive, motorsport, railway, or industrial automation)

Comfortable diagnosing and resolving technical issues on customer premises, often under time pressure

Strong communicator; able to translate complex technical information clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences

Genuinely comfortable with a role that is predominantly field-based across Australia and occasionally international

Valid Australian driver's licence and unrestricted passport

Strong electrical and mechanical diagnostic skills with impeccable attention to detail

Desirable:

Linux operating system experience

Familiarity with Jira, Confluence, Slack, or G-Suite

Root cause analysis methodologies

Background in autonomous systems or computer vision applications

Why Join Dexory?

You'll be Dexory's face in Australia - the person clients trust to deliver and maintain technology that genuinely transforms how their operations run. Hands-on, high-impact, and genuinely varied work with a world-class engineering team behind you.

Own something real: build and maintain Dexory's entire Australian deployment footprint from the ground up

Career growth: as our Australian presence expands, so does the opportunity around you

Competitive salary aligned to Australian market rates

Personal development budget

25 days annual leave plus Australian public holidays

Superannuation contributions in line with Australian statutory requirements

High-performance, low-ego team culture

Our Operating Principles

Performance: high standards, outstanding results | Impact: big challenges, bigger results | Commitment: all in, every time | One Team: one mission, shared success

We value humility as much as talent. If you meet most — but not all — of what we're looking for, please apply. Diversity of background drives better engineering and better outcomes for our clients.
